label: Yovelim (Jubilees) — Part 1 — Chapters 1–25
summary_plain: Yovelim is the Book of Jubilees — the retelling of all history from creation to Sinai, organized into jubilee periods of forty-nine years. YHWH speaks to Moshe on Sinai and commands him to write everything. The angel of the presence dictates the account from the heavenly tablets. The creation is retold with precise dating. The Shabbat is established as an eternal covenant sign. The fall, Qayin and Hevel, the Watchers, and the flood are all retold with jubilee-year dates. After the flood, Noakh's sons divide the earth. Demons attack their descendants. Noakh prays, and YHWH commands: bind them all. But Mastema, chief of the spirits, asks YHWH to leave a tenth under his authority — and YHWH allows it. The covenant with Avraham is retold with the 364-day solar calendar commanded. Circumcision on the eighth day is established as an eternal sign — written on the heavenly tablets. The Aqedah is attributed to Mastema's challenge.
summary_scholarly: Chapters 1 through 25 contain the Yovelim retelling of creation through the life of Avraham. The ACR reads from the Dead Sea Scrolls (4QJub-a through 4QJub-g, multiple Cave 4 and Cave 1 manuscripts) and the Orit Ge'ez which preserves the complete text — the only complete witness. The Yovelim text is structured as revelation: YHWH commands Moshe to write, and the angel of the presence dictates from the heavenly tablets (1:1, 2:1). Every event is dated to a specific jubilee, week of years, and year. The 364-day solar calendar is commanded in chapter 6: 'the year three hundred and sixty-four days' (6:32). Mastema in chapter 10 is the chief of the spirits — he negotiates with YHWH to retain authority over a tenth of the demons after the flood. The Aqedah in Yovelim is attributed to Mastema's challenge — paralleling the role of the Adversary. The circumcision covenant (ch 15) is tied to the heavenly tablets and the angels of the presence. The DSS attestation of Yovelim is extensive — more manuscripts than any non-Torah book except Chanokh.

key_terms.definition: The Book of Jubilees — named after the jubilee periods (49-year cycles) that structure all of history. European scholars called it Jubilees. The Orit Ge'ez preserves it complete.
The angel who dictates the Yovelim account to Moshe: 'the angel of the presence spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH' (2:1). He speaks from the heavenly tablets.
The source from which the angel of the presence reads. Everything in Yovelim — dates, laws, judgments — is recorded on the heavenly tablets. The same tablets Chanokh read in Chanokh 46:1.
A cycle of 49 years (seven weeks of years). All events in Yovelim are dated to a specific jubilee, week, and year — giving a precise chronological framework from creation to Sinai.

Chief of the spirits/demons. After the flood, YHWH commands all demons bound. Mastema asks YHWH to leave a tenth under his authority — and YHWH allows it (10:8-9). He later challenges YHWH to test Avraham through the Aqedah.
Yovelim commands the 364-day solar calendar: 'the year three hundred and sixty-four days' (6:32). This matches the Astronomical Book of Chanokh and the calendar of the Dead Sea Scrolls community.
In chapter 2, YHWH creates the Shabbat as an eternal sign: he rests on the seventh day and hallows it for all ages. The angels of the presence and the angels of sanctification keep Shabbat in heaven.

The covenant of circumcision is established in chapter 15 as an eternal sign written on the heavenly tablets. 'Everyone who is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the eighth day, belongs not to the children of the covenant' (15:25).
After the flood, Noakh's sons divide the earth by lot. Shem receives the center. Cham receives the south. Yefet receives the north. The boundaries are drawn and sworn to with an oath.

Multiple Qumran manuscripts preserve Yovelim: 4QJub-a through 4QJub-g plus Cave 1 and Cave 2 fragments. More DSS copies than any non-Torah book except Chanokh — showing its central importance at Qumran.

fill_blank.prompt: YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying: Come up to me on the mountain, and I will give you two tables of stone of the law and of the ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: And it came to pass in the first year of the exodus of the children of Yisra'EL out of Egypt, in the third month, on the sixteenth day of the month, that YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying: Come up to me on the mountain, and I will give you two tables of stone of the law and of the commandments, which I have written, that you may teach them.

fill_blank.prompt: And the angel of the ______ spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH.
fill_blank.source_quote: And the angel of the presence spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH, saying: Write the complete history of the creation.

fill_blank.prompt: They will not make the year three hundred and ______-four days.
fill_blank.source_quote: For this reason I command and testify to you that you may testify to them — for after your death your children will disturb them, so that they will not make the year three hundred and sixty-four days.

fill_blank.prompt: And the chief of the spirits, ______, came and said: YHWH Creator, Creator, let some of them remain before me.
fill_blank.source_quote: And the chief of the spirits, Mastema, came and said: YHWH Creator, Creator, let some of them remain before me, and let them hearken to my voice.

fill_blank.prompt: Everyone who is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the ______ day, belongs not to the children of the covenant.
fill_blank.source_quote: And everyone who is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the eighth day, belongs not to the children of the covenant which YHWH made with Avraham.

fill_blank.prompt: And Moshe was on the mountain forty days and forty nights, and YHWH taught him the earlier and the later history of the division of all the days of the law and of the ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: And Moshe was on the mountain forty days and forty nights, and YHWH taught him the earlier and the later history of the division of all the days of the law and of the testimony.

fill_blank.prompt: Incline your heart to every word which I shall speak to you on this mountain, and write them in a ______ in order.
fill_blank.source_quote: And he said: Incline your heart to every word which I shall speak to you on this mountain, and write them in a book in order that their generations may see how I have not forsaken them.

fill_blank.prompt: In the third week in the second jubilee, Qa'in slew Hevel because YHWH accepted Hevel's ______ but Qa'in's sacrifice he did not accept.
fill_blank.source_quote: And in the third week in the second jubilee, Qa'in slew Hevel because YHWH accepted Hevel's sacrifice but Qa'in's sacrifice he did not accept.

fill_blank.prompt: And YHWH our Creator bade us to ______ all.
fill_blank.source_quote: And YHWH our Creator bade us to bind all.

fill_blank.prompt: I know their rebellion and their stiff ______, before I bring them into the land.
fill_blank.source_quote: And do you write for yourself all these words which I declare to you this day, for I know their rebellion and their stiff neck, before I bring them into the land of which I swore to their fathers.

multiple_choice.question: Who dictates the Yovelim account to Moshe?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And the angel of the presence spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH, saying: Write the complete history of the creation.

multiple_choice.question: How many days does the Yovelim calendar have?
multiple_choice.source_quote: They will not make the year three hundred and sixty-four days.

multiple_choice.question: What did Mastema ask YHWH for after the flood?
options: To leave a tenth of the demons under his authority
To rebuild the tower of Bavel
To become an angel of the presence
multiple_choice.source_quote: And the chief of the spirits, Mastema, came and said: YHWH Creator, Creator, let some of them remain before me, and let them hearken to my voice.

multiple_choice.question: Where does YHWH speak to Moshe at the beginning of Yovelim?
multiple_choice.source_quote: YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying: Come up to me on the mountain.

multiple_choice.question: When must circumcision take place according to the heavenly tablets?
multiple_choice.source_quote: Everyone who is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the eighth day, belongs not to the children of the covenant.

multiple_choice.question: What did YHWH teach Moshe on the mountain for forty days?
options: The earlier and later history of all the days of the law and testimony
multiple_choice.source_quote: And YHWH taught him the earlier and the later history of the division of all the days of the law and of the testimony.

multiple_choice.question: What are jubilee periods?
options: Cycles of 49 years (seven weeks of years)
multiple_choice.source_quote: Named after the jubilee periods (49-year cycles) that structure all of history.

multiple_choice.question: Who attacked the children of Noakh after the flood?
multiple_choice.source_quote: And in the third week of this jubilee the unclean demons began to lead astray the children of the sons of Noakh, and to make to err and destroy them.

multiple_choice.question: How many DSS manuscripts preserve Yovelim?
options: More than any non-Torah book except Chanokh
multiple_choice.source_quote: More manuscripts than any non-Torah book except Chanokh — showing its central importance at Qumran.

multiple_choice.question: How does Yovelim date the killing of Hevel by Qa'in?
options: The first year of the first jubilee
The third week in the second jubilee
The seventh week in the third jubilee
The first month of the tenth jubilee
multiple_choice.source_quote: And in the third week in the second jubilee, Qa'in slew Hevel.

faq.question: What is Yovelim?
faq.answer: The Book of Jubilees — a retelling of all history from creation to Sinai, organized into jubilee periods of 49 years. YHWH speaks to Moshe on Sinai and the angel of the presence dictates from the heavenly tablets. Every event is dated precisely. The text is preserved complete in the Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community and attested in multiple Dead Sea Scrolls manuscripts from Qumran.

faq.question: Who is Mastema?
faq.answer: The chief of the spirits — the demons that remained after the flood. YHWH commanded all demons bound (10:8). Mastema negotiated: 'Let some of them remain before me, and let them hearken to my voice' (10:9). YHWH allowed a tenth to remain under Mastema's authority. Mastema later tests Avraham through the Aqedah — this is the Yovelim backstory that Bereshit 22 does not provide. Mastema is the adversary figure in Yovelim.

faq.question: Why is the 364-day calendar important in Yovelim?
faq.answer: Yovelim commands the 364-day solar calendar in chapter 6: the year is 364 days, four seasons of 91 days. This matches the Astronomical Book of Chanokh (ACR Volume 6 Part 2) and the calendar used by the Qumran community. The text warns that after Moshe's death his children will 'disturb' the calendar — the implication is that a different calendar (the 354-day lunar calendar later adopted in rabbinic practice) is a deviation from what was written on the heavenly tablets.

faq.question: What are the heavenly tablets in Yovelim?
faq.answer: The source of everything the angel of the presence dictates. All laws, all dates, all judgments, the covenant of circumcision — everything is 'written on the heavenly tablets.' The same tablets Chanokh reads in Chanokh 46:1. Yovelim treats the heavenly tablets as the ultimate authority behind the Torah.

faq.question: Why are there so many DSS copies of Yovelim?
faq.answer: Yovelim is one of the most frequently copied texts at Qumran — 4QJub-a through 4QJub-g plus Cave 1 and Cave 2 fragments. Only Chanokh has more non-Torah copies. This shows that the Qumran community treated Yovelim as central scripture — not secondary, not commentary, but primary. The Orit Ge'ez community likewise preserves it as part of the canon.

faq.question: How does Yovelim retell the Aqedah?
faq.answer: In Yovelim, the Aqedah (the binding of Yitzhak) is attributed to Mastema's challenge. Mastema approaches YHWH and says Avraham loves Yitzhak more than anything — test him. YHWH commands the test. This provides the backstory that Bereshit 22 states only as 'The Creator tested Avraham.' The Yovelim version places the Aqedah in a specific jubilee year and ties it to Mastema's ongoing role as the adversary.

faq.question: What is the mathematical proof for the 364-day solar calendar?
faq.answer: 364 divided by 7 equals exactly 52 weeks. Every date falls on the same day of the week every year. The New Year (1 Abib) always begins on Wednesday — the 4th day — because that is the day the luminaries were created. The flood narrative provides the mathematical proof: Bereshit 7:11 states the flood began on the 17th of the 2nd month. Bereshit 8:3-4 states the waters receded after 150 days on the 17th of the 7th month. Five months equals 150 days — exactly 30 days per month. The Masoretic lunar month alternates between 29 and 30 days, producing 147 days for the same period — a 3-day discrepancy with the Torah's own stated number. The Torah's arithmetic resolves only within the 364-day solar calendar.

faq.question: Why does Yovelim condemn the lunar calendar?
faq.answer: Yovelim 6:32-38 states that those who follow the moon will 'err in seasons, years, and appointed times' and 'forget the Sabbaths.' Calendar observance is framed as covenant violation at the level of idolatry. The Rabbinical calendar currently in use was standardized by Hillel II approximately 359 CE using the 19-year Babylonian Metonic cycle with Babylonian month names (Nisan, Tammuz, Elul). The Qumran community separated from the Jerusalem Temple authorities specifically because they believed the calendar had been corrupted by switching to a Babylonian lunar system. The document 4QMMT lists calendar observance as a primary point of dispute.

faq.question: What does Yovelim 23 say about restoration?
faq.answer: Yovelim 23:26 (confirmed in Qumran fragments): 'And in those days the children shall begin to study the laws and to seek the commandments and to return to the path of righteousness.' Yovelim 23:27: 'And YHWH will heal His servants and they shall rise up and see great peace and drive out their adversaries.' The human action — returning to study of the ancient laws directly — is the ignition point that produces the divine response. The restoration is activated by the covenant people returning to their own texts. The generation currently accessing the Dead Sea Scrolls, 1 Chanokh, Yovelim, and the Orit Ge'ez as primary sources — bypassing both Rabbinical and Christian institutional frameworks — is the first such generation in 1,900 years.

faq.question: What does Yovelim say about the nations and angelic governance?
faq.answer: Yovelim 15 documents that every nation on earth was assigned to angelic governors — beings from the divine council given jurisdiction over specific peoples. Yisra'EL alone was kept under YHWH's direct governance — not assigned to any angelic intermediary. This direct governance is defined as the definition of the covenant itself. When the covenant people violate covenant terms, they fall out of YHWH's direct jurisdiction and come under the governance of the angelic rulers of other nations — which manifests as conquest, slavery, and exile.
